text:
Admission Date:  1988-07-12              Discharge Date:   1997-05-11

Date of Birth:  2010-05-28             Sex:   M

Service: SURGERY

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Ricky
Chief Complaint:
s/p MVC

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None.


History of Present Illness:
47 year old unrestrained driver, reported to cross the
centerline, with head on collision with another vehicle.  Broken
windshield.  Patient found on floor of passenger seat.  + LOC  +
EtOH

Past Medical History:
None.

Social History:
Per family patient has history of alcohol abuse

Family History:
NC

Physical Exam:
99.0, 84, 144/63, 18, 99% RA.
A&O x 3, GCS 15
HEENT: Airway intact, 3 cm laceration/abrasion on occiput,
pupils 3-->2 mm bilaterally, TM clear, dentition intact.
CV: RRR
Chest: CTAB, equal chest excursion
Abd: soft, diffusely non-tender, FAST negative
Back: reported pain along entire thoracic spine
Pelvis: stable
Ext: R knee laceration/abrasion, minor R knee abrasions, equal
DP pulses bilaterally

Pertinent Results:
Admission labs:
WBC-14.0* RBC-5.01 HGB-14.8 HCT-39.6* MCV-79* PLT COUNT-261
PT-11.9 PTT-22.0 INR(PT)-1.0
FIBRINOGE-275
GLUCOSE-119* LACTATE-4.7* NA+-136 K+-3.8 CL--94* TCO2-29
UreaN-14 Creat-1.1
Amylase-38
ASA-NEG Ethanol-305* Acetmnp-NEG Bnzodzp-NEG Barbitr-NEG
Tricycl-NEG
URINE  BLOOD-SM NITRITE-NEG PROTEIN-TR GLUCOSE-NEG KETONE-15
BILIRUBIN-NEG UROBILNGN-0.2 PH-5.5 LEUK-NEG COLOR-Yellow
APPEAR-Clear SP Arnold-1.018
URINE  bnzodzpn-NEG barbitrt-NEG opiates-NEG cocaine-NEG
amphetmn-NEG mthdone-NEG

Follow-up labs:
07-13: BLOOD WBC-7.8 RBC-4.47* Hgb-13.0* Hct-35.5* MCV-79*
MCH-29.0 MCHC-36.6* RDW-12.6 Plt Ct-175 Plt Ct-175  Ethanol-NEG

Imaging:
CT HEAD W/O CONTRAST  1988-07-12 9:33 AM CONCLUSION: Poor quality
study due to patient motion. No definite signs for intracranial
hemorrhage.

CT C-SPINE W/O CONTRAST  1988-07-12 9:34 AM IMPRESSION: No
fracture or malalignment. Congenital widening of the
interlaminar angle leading to a narrowed canal. Additionally,
severe spondylosis of the cervical spine including anterior
wedging of the C5 vertebra, osteophyte formation at C3 through
C7, and disc osteophyte complexes at C3-7, that additionally
narrow the spinal canal.

TRAUMA #2 (AP CXR & PELVIS PORT)  1988-07-12 9:29 AM IMPRESSION:
Unremarkable portable chest x-ray.

CT CHEST, ABD, PELVIS W/CONTRAST  1988-07-12 9:35 AM IMPRESSION:1.
No evidence of traumatic injury to the chest, abdomen or pelvis.
2. Focal small low density liver lesions within the right lobe
that are too small to characterize, which probably represent
hemangiomas. Recommend followup ultrasound scan (nonemergently)
for more complete evaluation. 3. Small hiatal hernia.

KNEE (2 VIEWS) RIGHT  IMPRESSION: No evidence of acute fracture
or dislocation. Small right knee joint effusion.
Chondrocalcinosis.

Brief Hospital Course:
Neuro: Initially imaging was negative for acute traumatic
injury, however even after no longer intoxicated, Mr. Kirby
had signficant difficulties with attention and short term
memory.  Behavioral Neurology was consulted and recommended
inpatient treatment with Seroquel and ambien; and noted it may
take months to return to baseline function.  By HD #3 Mr.
Young short term memory was substantially improved; he
persisted to have some difficulties with concentration, however
was deemed safe for discharge to home.  He will follow-up in the
trauma clinic and with Behavioral Neurology.

EtOH: Mr. Kirby was counseled repeatedly regarding his
alcohol abuse and the risk for future personal injury and injury
to others with continued EtOH abuse.

Medications on Admission:
None.

Discharge Medications:
1. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO Q6H (every 6
hours) as needed for headache,fever.
2. Ibuprofen 400 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO Q8H (every 8
hours) as needed for pain.


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
Motor Vehicle Collision
Concussion
Transient short term memory difficulties


Discharge Condition:
Good.  Ambulating well with improvement in short term memory.


Discharge Instructions:
You were admitted to the River Falls Area Hsptl on 1988-07-12 after a motor
vehicle collision.  You initially had some difficulties with
short term memory, however your memory returned to near normal
baseline by the time of discharge on 07-26.  You were noted
to have some difficulties with concentration, too.  Imaging was
obtained of your head, neck, chest, and were negative for acute
injury (including fractures or head bleeds).

You should take all medications as prescribed.

You should follow-up as indicated below.


Followup Instructions:
You should be seen in the Trauma Clinic in 1 week (call
498-337-1215 to make an appointment).

You should have a follow-up appointment with Behavioral
Neurology to follow your memory and concentration difficulties;
in the hospital you were seen by Dr. James Nelson.  Call
them at (772-250-5563 to make an appointment.

You should contact your primary care physician and notify
him/her of your hospitalization.

On your abdomen scan there were some small lesions (spots) on
your liver that were too small to characterize, possibly a
hemangioma (benign growth/collection of blood vessels).  We
recommend followup ultrasound scan (nonemergently) for more
complete evaluation.



Completed by:1997-05-11
